title: 2010 Business Computers Leave the Starting Gate
author: Peter S. Kastner
abstract: Article from the Daily Globe (Hillsboro, OR) reviewing Intel's 2010 vPro technology launch for business PCs. Covers Core i5/i7 processors built on 32nm process technology, featuring hardware encryption (AES-NI), advanced power management saving 1-2 kWh per shift, and vPro remote management capabilities. Discusses ecosystem partners VMware, Symantec, Citrix, and Microsoft, plus new models from Lenovo, Dell, HP, and Acer. Examines PC ownership costs ($500-$2,500/user/year) and Intel's push to extend enterprise management tools to SMBs.
importance_rationale: Solid tech journalism covering a significant Intel product launch with hands-on testing context, but one of many vPro articles from the period
relevance_rationale: vPro concepts of remote management and hardware-level security remain highly relevant to modern endpoint management; specific hardware generations are now historical
prescience_rationale: Correctly predicted hardware encryption (AES-NI) becoming a universal standard, foresaw remote management becoming essential for distributed workforces years before the 2020 remote work explosion, and anticipated SMBs adopting enterprise-grade management tools
